title: Guardrails & safety: input/output filters, jailbreaks, red-teaming
focus: Defense-in-depth; classify the major jailbreak categories.

items.label: Valid Parenthesis String
interviewQuestions: Prove the greedy choice — why is the locally-optimal pick safe globally? (Exchange argument or staying-ahead.)
When does greedy fail on a similar-looking problem, and what would you reach for instead (DP, BFS)?
Walk through edge cases that often break naive greedy: ties, negatives, single element.

items.label: Input filtering & output moderation
interviewQuestions: Compare input vs output filtering — what does each catch and miss?
What latency does an output-moderation step add, and how do you keep it under your SLO?

items.label: Jailbreaks: DAN, role-play, payload smuggling
interviewQuestions: Walk through three categories of jailbreak attacks, and how you'd defend against each.
Why is many-shot jailbreaking so effective on long-context models, and what mitigates it?

items.label: Red-teaming & evals for safety
interviewQuestions: How would you build a red-teaming process for a customer-facing LLM?
How would you scale red-teaming with automated attackers without missing novel attack vectors?
